---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-worldmap3.png')
background-size: 105%
background-class: top

<br>

.size60[

**Today's Agenda: Final Paper Prep**

- Strengthening explanations and analyses
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
]]

???

## Prep for class
1. Review submitted work on Canvas (notes from applying models to the cases)

2. *For you:* 10 mins per exercise (event, model 1, model 2, model 3) THEN add exercise focused on identifying your thesis. 





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.content-box-purple[**Final Exam (Due Dec 12, 10am)**]]

.size35[
Select a **NEW** international political event and make an argument that one of the IR theories / models we've studied in class this term "best" explains the event. 

Your paper must consider and evaluate how **THREE** of the IR theories from class explain your chosen event.

- No double-dipping on realism/liberalism

- At least one theory from the second half of class

- No events used in the prior paper
]

???

### Any questions on the final paper prompt?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size45[
.content-box-white[**For Today:**]

1. Describe your chosen international political event (cite evidence), 

2. Use each selected model (x3) to explain your event, and

3. Evaluate each model in terms of its strengths and weaknesses (minimum 3 each).
]

???

On Monday we made sure everyone had a clear event and had selected three models

<br>

Today we make sure each of the three components of the final paper are in really good shape.

1. Can you clearly describe your international political event?
    - And do you have high quality evidence of the basic facts?
    
2. Can you explain the event using each of your three models
    - Is each explanation complete and coherent?
    
3. Can you evaluate the strengths and weaknesses of each explanation?
    - e.g. provide multiple reasons someone might prefer one model over the others?
    
<br>

### Do our goals for today make sense?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/15_2-international_flags_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.size50[
.center[**Describe the Event**]

1. Is this a single, specific event?

2. Is it international?

3. Is it political?

4. Are the basic facts established by high quality evidence?
]

???

Pair off and review the event!

- Make sure you are convinced the other person has convincingly addressed these four things.





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/15_2-international_flags_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.size50[
.center[**Explain the Event with ONE Model**]

1. Fully explain your event

2. What are the strongest parts of the explanation?

3. What are the weakest parts of the explanation?
]

???

Change up the pairings!

- Walk the other person through you applying ONE of your models to the event

- Other person, help them flesh out or deepen each of these three pieces.

<br>

Swap up your pairings and now do your second model

- Go!

<br>

Swap it up again and do your final model

- Go!

<br>

Ok, time to reflect on your work today.

- I'd be shocked if pitching someone each model hasn't changed your thinking about them

- PLUS their feedback must have given you something of use!

<br>

Take five minutes to decide on the thesis for your paper

- Which model is the most useful? Why?

- Jot down some notes!







---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.pull-left[

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**For Friday**]]

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**Draft an Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size30[**Intro**
+ What is the question?
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis statement
]

.size30[**Apply Theory 1**
+ Evidence
]

.size30[**Apply Theory 2**
+ Evidence
]

.size30[**Apply Theory 3**
+ Evidence
]]


???

Outline due in class on Friday

- Submit as an assignment, NOT a Canvas discussion posting

- Bring your outline to class

<br>

### Questions?

- Get to it!
